What is the encapsulation?
opt1: Bundling data and methods that operate on that data within a single unit (class), while restricting direct access to some components.
opt2: Hiding internal state and requiring all interaction to be performed through an object's methods.
opt3: Protecting data integrity by preventing external code from directly modifying internal variables.
opt4: Using access modifiers (like private and public) to expose only necessary functionality and conceal implementation details.
answer: Bundling data and methods that operate on that data within a single unit (class), while restricting direct access to some components.
